allow:
Bash(curl -sI "https://commtrans-gtfs.azurewebsites.net/api/Download" 2>&1 | grep -i "http/\\|content-type\\|location"
curl -sI "https://commtrans-gtfs.azurewebsites.net/api/gtfsfeed" 2>&1 | grep -i "http/\\|content-type\\|location"
curl -sI "https://commtrans-gtfs.azurewebsites.net/api/zip" 2>&1 | grep -i "http/\\|content-type\\|location")

Bash(node -e "const d = JSON.parse\(require\('fs'\).readFileSync\('public/pulse-data.json','utf8'\)\); const s = d.systems?.find\(s => s.id?.includes\('swift'\) || s.title?.includes\('Swift'\) || s.kicker?.includes\('Swift'\)\); console.log\(JSON.stringify\(s?.lines?.map\(l => \({id:l.id, name:l.name}\)\), null, 2\)\)")

Bash(~/.claude/bin/codeagent-wrapper --backend gemini --gemini-model gemini-3-pro-preview - "$PWD" <<'EOF'
ROLE_FILE: ~/.claude/.ccg/prompts/gemini/analyzer.md
<TASK>
Requirement: Design a "Station Hotspot" section for the Insights tab of a real-time transit tracking PWA \(Link Pulse\). This feature should highlight stations with the most delays or largest service gaps passing through them.

Context:
- The app tracks Seattle transit \(Link light rail, RapidRide buses, Swift buses\)
- Each vehicle has: currentStopId, currentStopLabel, scheduleDeviation \(seconds\), closestStop, nextStop, directionSymbol
- Each line has stations \(stops\) with: id, label, y position, cumulativeMinutes, segmentMinutes
- Vehicles are stored in state.vehiclesByLine \(Map<lineId, Vehicle[]>\)
- Layouts with stations are in state.layouts \(Map<lineId, Layout>\)
- The Insights tab already has: system summary card, per-line cards with headway/delay/flow, ticker, clickable detail dialogs
- The app supports bilingual \(en/zh-CN\) and dark/light themes
- Built with vanilla JS, no framework - HTML string rendering
- Existing design uses: metric-chip cards, flow-lanes, delay-chips, insight-exceptions
- The station hotspot should aggregate vehicle delay data per station across all visible lines

Provide at least 2 solution approaches for the Station Hotspot UI:
1. How to visualize which stations are "hot" \(high delay, large gaps\)
2. What metrics to show per station
3. Where it should go in the Insights tab layout
4. Interaction patterns \(click behavior\)
5. How it works on mobile vs tablet
</TASK>
OUTPUT: UI feasibility analysis, at least 2 recommended solutions with mockups, UX evaluation
EOF)
